title: Paul's Second Missionary Journey
description: A modest Acts-based route from Syria through Asia Minor into Macedonia and Greece.
caution: The route follows Acts closely but still compresses overland segments and includes reconstructed waypoints.

stops.name: Antioch in Syria
stops.description: The sending church for the second journey.

stops.name: Derbe
stops.description: A revisited inland stop in Asia Minor.
stops.notes: Reused from the first journey as a continuation point.

stops.name: Troas
stops.notes: Acts gives this stop a key narrative role.

stops.name: Philippi
stops.description: An important Macedonian city in the mission.

stops.name: Thessalonica
stops.description: A major city where the message spread.
stops.notes: A core missionary city in Acts.

stops.name: Athens
stops.notes: A key location for the sermon to the philosophers.

stops.name: Corinth
stops.description: A major ministry center in Greece.
stops.notes: Acts and Paul's letters both highlight Corinth.

segments.label: Revisiting the churches
segments.description: Paul and Silas strengthen the churches.
segments.caution: The inland route is highly compressed.

segments.label: Toward the sea
segments.description: The mission moves west to the coast.
segments.caution: A representative line stands for the route through Asia Minor.

segments.label: Crossing to Macedonia
segments.description: The Macedonian call sends the team across the Aegean.

segments.label: Macedonian corridor
segments.description: The journey continues westward across Macedonia.
segments.caution: The Egnatian road is represented schematically.

segments.label: Into Achaia
segments.description: The mission heads south into Greece.
segments.caution: The overland route is not fully specified in Acts.

segments.label: Achaian ministry
segments.description: Paul moves from Athens to Corinth.
segments.caution: A direct line summarizes the regional move.
